quote: You have power over your mind - not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Everything we hear is an opinion, not a fact. Everything we see is a perspective, not the truth.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Dwell on the beauty of life. Watch the stars, and see yourself running with them.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: If you are distressed by anything external, the pain is not due to the thing itself but to your estimate of it; and this you have the power to revoke at any moment.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The best revenge is to be unlike him who performed the injury.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: It is not death that a man should fear, but he should fear never beginning to live.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Accept the things to which fate binds you, and love the people with whom fate brings you together, but do so with all your heart.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Never let the future disturb you. You will meet it, if you have to, with the same weapons of reason which today arm you against the present.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Whenever you are about to find fault with someone, ask yourself the following question: What fault of mine most nearly resembles the one I am about to criticize?
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: If someone is able to show me that what I think or do is not right, I will happily change, for I seek the truth, by which no one was ever truly harmed.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: To stand up straight - not straightened.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Concentrate on this, your whole life long: for your mind to be in the right state.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: No random actions, none not based on underlying principles.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: There's nothing manly about rage.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Everyone gets one life. Don't waste it being someone you are not.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Not to live as if you had endless years ahead of you. Death overshadows you.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Why all this guesswork? You can see what needs to be done. If you can see the road, follow it cheerfully, without turning back.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Don't you see how much you have to offer, beyond excuses like 'can't'? And yet you still settle for less.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: If it is not right, do not do it. If it is not true, do not say it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Very little is needed to make a happy life; it is all within yourself, in your way of thinking.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The universe is change; our life is what our thoughts make it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: He who lives in harmony with himself lives in harmony with the universe.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: To live happily is an inward power of the soul.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Poverty is the mother of crime.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: It is not the man who has too little, but the man who craves more, that is poor.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Confine yourself to the present.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Loss is nothing else but change, and change is Nature's delight.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Let it be your constant method to look into the design of people's actions, and see what they would be at, as often as it is practicable.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: How much trouble he avoids who does not look to see what his neighbor says or does or thinks.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: When you wake up in the morning, tell yourself: the people I deal with today will be meddling, ungrateful, arrogant, dishonest, jealous, and surly.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Our actions may be impeded, but there can be no impeding our intentions or dispositions, because we can accommodate and adapt.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: If you seek tranquility, do less. Ask yourself at every moment, is this necessary?
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: To feel affection for people even when they make mistakes is uniquely human.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Remember that man lives only in the present, in this fleeting instant; all the rest of his life is either past and gone, or not yet revealed.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Be like the rocky headland on which the waves constantly break. It stands firm, and round it the seething waters are calmed.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: How quickly things vanish: in the universe, the bodies themselves, but in time, the memory of them.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Nowhere can a man find a quieter or more untroubled retreat than in his own soul.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Such as are your habitual thoughts, such also will be the character of your mind; for the soul is dyed by the thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: A man's worth is no greater than the worth of his ambitions.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Be tolerant with others and strict with yourself.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Look well into thyself; there is a source of strength which will always spring up if thou wilt always look there.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: It is in your power to withdraw yourself whenever you desire.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Tomorrow is nothing, today is too late; the good lived yesterday.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Reject your sense of injury and the injury itself disappears.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do every act of your life as if it were your last.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Time is a sort of river of passing events, and strong is its current; no sooner is a thing brought to sight than it is swept by and another takes its place.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Adapt yourself to the things among which your lot has been cast, and the men among whom you live, love them, but do it truly, sincerely.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Misfortune nobly born is good fortune.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Disturbance comes only from within, from one's own perceptions.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The art of living is more like wrestling than dancing, because it requires being prepared to meet and withstand sudden and unexpected attacks.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Whatever any one does or says, I must be good; just as if the gold, or the emerald, or the purple were always saying this: whatever any one does or says, I must be emerald and keep my color.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Be not ashamed to be helped; for it is thy business to do thy duty like a soldier in the assault on a town.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Nothing happens to anybody which he is not formed by nature to bear.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Receive without pride, let go without attachment.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a lot of it. Life is long enough if it were all well invested.
author: Seneca

quote: We are not given a short life but we make it short, and we are not ill-supplied but wasteful of it.
author: Seneca

quote: People are frugal in guarding their personal property, but as soon as it comes to squandering time, they are most wasteful of the one thing in which it is right to be stingy.
author: Seneca

quote: You act like mortals in all that you fear, and like immortals in all that you desire.
author: Seneca

quote: How late it is to begin really to live just when life must end.
author: Seneca

quote: It is inevitable that life will be not just very short but very miserable for those who acquire by great toil what they must keep by greater toil.
author: Seneca

quote: As far as I am concerned, I know that I have lost not wealth but distractions.
author: Seneca

quote: Nothing satisfies greed, but even a little satisfies nature.
author: Seneca

quote: The whole future lies in uncertainty: live immediately.
author: Seneca

quote: But learning how to live takes a whole life, and, which may surprise you more, it takes a whole life to learn how to die.
author: Seneca

quote: He who fears death will never do anything worthy of a living man.
author: Seneca

quote: All those who call you to themselves draw you away from yourself.
author: Seneca

quote: Of all people only those are at leisure who make time for philosophy; only those are really alive.
author: Seneca

quote: Putting things off is the biggest waste of life: it snatches away each day as it comes, and denies us the present by promising the future.
author: Seneca

quote: The greatest obstacle to living is expectancy, which hangs upon tomorrow and loses today.
author: Seneca

quote: So you must match time's swiftness with your speed in using it.
author: Seneca

quote: We are tight-fisted with property and money, yet think too little of wasting time, the one thing about which we should all be the toughest misers.
author: Seneca

quote: No condition is so bitter that a stable mind cannot find some consolation in it.
author: Seneca

quote: We suffer more in imagination than in reality.
author: Seneca

quote: Difficulties strengthen the mind, as labor does the body.
author: Seneca

quote: Sometimes even to live is an act of courage.
author: Seneca

quote: He who is brave is free.
author: Seneca

quote: Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity.
author: Seneca

quote: Every new beginning comes from some other beginning's end.
author: Seneca

quote: It is the power of the mind to be unconquerable.
author: Seneca

quote: Hang on to your youthful enthusiasms; you'll be able to use them better when you're older.
author: Seneca

quote: Wealth is the slave of a wise man and the master of a fool.
author: Seneca

quote: The mind that is anxious about future events is miserable.
author: Seneca

quote: If a man knows not to which port he sails, no wind is favorable.
author: Seneca

quote: A gem cannot be polished without friction, nor a man perfected without trials.
author: Seneca

quote: True happiness is to enjoy the present, without anxious dependence upon the future.
author: Seneca

quote: While we wait for life, life passes.
author: Seneca

quote: Man cannot be deprived of the things that truly belong to him.
author: Seneca

quote: Begin at once to live, and count each separate day as a separate life.
author: Seneca

quote: The mind freed from the usual ways of looking at things acquires a wonderful breadth of vision.
author: Seneca

quote: Fire is the test of gold; adversity, of strong men.
author: Seneca

quote: Associate with people who are likely to improve you.
author: Seneca

quote: Religion is regarded by the common people as true, by the wise as false, and by the rulers as useful.
author: Seneca

quote: Nothing is more honorable than a grateful heart.
author: Seneca

quote: We are all chained to fortune; the chain of one is made of gold and wide, while that of another is short and rusty. But what difference does it make?
author: Seneca

quote: Travel and change of place impart new vigor to the mind.
author: Seneca

quote: Let your tears flow, but let them also cease.
author: Seneca

quote: To wish to be well is a part of becoming well.
author: Seneca

quote: What need is there to weep over parts of life? The whole of it calls for tears.
author: Seneca

quote: It is not that we have so little time but that we lose so much.
author: Seneca

quote: Anger, if not restrained, is frequently more hurtful to us than the injury that provokes it.
author: Seneca

quote: A man who suffers before it is necessary, suffers more than is necessary.
author: Seneca

quote: Many men get knowledge, but very few wisdom.
author: Seneca

quote: The bravest sight in the world is to see a great man struggling against adversity.
author: Seneca

quote: Sometimes the very fear of suffering makes men suffer.
author: Seneca

quote: To be everywhere is to be nowhere.
author: Seneca

quote: In every good there is admixture of bad, and in every bad there is some seed of good.
author: Seneca

quote: No man was ever wise by chance.
author: Seneca

quote: Until we have begun to go without them, we fail to realize how unnecessary many things are.
author: Seneca

quote: It is a denial of justice not to stretch out a helping hand to the fallen.
author: Seneca

quote: To everything, time brings degeneration, but knowledge, too, can be transmitted.
author: Seneca

quote: As long as you live, keep learning how to live.
author: Seneca

quote: Sometimes that which has been most carefully concealed becomes most evident.
author: Seneca

quote: A great fortune is a great slavery.
author: Seneca

quote: It isn't the things themselves that disturb people, but the judgments that they form about them.
author: Epictetus

quote: Men are disturbed not by the things that happen, but by their opinions about the things that happen.
author: Epictetus

quote: No man is free who is not master of himself.
author: Epictetus

quote: It is impossible for a man to learn what he thinks he already knows.
author: Epictetus

quote: Who is your master? Anyone who has control over things upon which you've set your heart, or over things which you seek to avoid.
author: Epictetus

quote: You are not your body and hair-style, but your capacity for choosing well.
author: Epictetus

quote: We are not privy to the stories behind people's actions, so we should be patient with others and suspend judgment of them.
author: Epictetus

quote: In prosperity it is very easy to find a friend; but in adversity it is the most difficult of all things.
author: Epictetus

quote: Deliberate much before saying or doing anything, for you will not have the power of recalling what is said or done.
author: Epictetus

quote: To make the best of what is in our power, and take the rest as it occurs.
author: Epictetus

quote: A ship should not ride on a single anchor, nor life on a single hope.
author: Epictetus

quote: There is only one way to happiness, and that is to cease worrying about things which are beyond the power of our will.
author: Epictetus

quote: An ignorant person is inclined to blame others for his own misfortune. To blame oneself is proof of progress.
author: Epictetus

quote: If you have assumed a character above your strength, you have both acted in an unbecoming way and neglected that which you might have fulfilled.
author: Epictetus

quote: Circumstances do not make the man; they merely reveal him to himself.
author: Epictetus

quote: We have two ears and one mouth so that we can listen twice as much as we speak.
author: Epictetus

quote: It takes more than just a good-looking body. You've got to have the heart and soul to go with it.
author: Epictetus

quote: Some things are in our control and others not. Things in our control are opinion, pursuit, desire, aversion.
author: Epictetus

quote: Only the educated are free.
author: Epictetus

quote: First say to yourself what you would be, and then do what you have to do.
author: Epictetus

quote: Wealth consists not in having great possessions, but in having few wants.
author: Epictetus

quote: Don't explain your philosophy. Embody it.
author: Epictetus

quote: It is the act of an ill-instructed man to blame others for his own bad condition.
author: Epictetus

quote: Freedom is the only worthy goal in life. It is won by disregarding things that lie beyond our control.
author: Epictetus

quote: If you want to improve, be content to be thought foolish and stupid.
author: Epictetus

quote: How long are you going to wait before you demand the best for yourself?
author: Epictetus

quote: Whenever you are angry, be assured that it is not only a present evil, but that you have increased a habit.
author: Epictetus

quote: Man is disturbed not by events but by the views he takes of them.
author: Epictetus

quote: Caretake this moment. Immerse yourself in its particulars.
author: Epictetus

quote: Difficulties are things that show a person what they are.
author: Epictetus

quote: Demand not that events should happen as you wish, but wish them to happen as they do happen, and you will go on well.
author: Epictetus

quote: He who hangs his happiness on the actions or opinions of others will never be content.
author: Epictetus

quote: Any person capable of angering you becomes your master.
author: Epictetus

quote: Keep death and exile before your eyes each day, along with everything that seems terrible, and you will never have a base thought.
author: Epictetus

quote: A room without books is like a body without a soul.
author: Cicero

quote: While there's life, there's hope.
author: Cicero

quote: The life given us, by nature is short, but the memory of a well-spent life is eternal.
author: Cicero

quote: Nothing is so unbearable as ease of mind, unless it is alleviated by alternating exertion.
author: Cicero

quote: Virtue is the only true and substantial greatness.
author: Cicero

quote: To be ignorant of what occurred before you were born is to remain perpetually a child.
author: Cicero

quote: The greater the difficulty, the greater the glory.
author: Cicero

quote: Friendship makes prosperity more shining and lessens adversity by dividing and sharing it.
author: Cicero

quote: Old age, especially an honored old age, has so great authority, that this is of more value than all the pleasures of youth.
author: Cicero

quote: Gratitude is not only the greatest of virtues, but the parent of all the others.
author: Cicero

quote: An honest man can never become an outcast wherever he goes.
author: Cicero

quote: Glory follows virtue as if it were its shadow.
author: Cicero

quote: It is the nature of every person to err, but only the fool perseveres in error.
author: Cicero

quote: Within the character of the citizen lies the welfare of the nation.
author: Cicero

quote: A man of courage is also full of faith.
author: Cicero

quote: The pleasures of the table are shared by all ranks; it is only the means and not the gusto for them, that fortune varies.
author: Cicero

quote: No one can give you wiser advice than yourself.
author: Cicero

quote: Man conquers the world by conquering himself.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: The goal of life is living in agreement with nature.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: Happiness is a good flow of life.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: Better to trip with the feet than with the tongue.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: Well-being is realized by small steps, but is truly no small thing.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: Man's happiness consists in living agreeably to nature.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: Lead me, O Zeus, and thou, O Destiny, wheresoever I am appointed to go, and I will follow without wavering.
author: Cleanthes

quote: Fate guides the willing, but drags the unwilling.
author: Cleanthes

quote: If you accomplish something good with hard work, the labor passes quickly, but the good endures.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: There is value in every type of work that is honest and productive.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: It is impossible to live well today unless one treats it as one's last day.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: To live well is the most important thing, and philosophy is nothing other than this: investigating how one might live well.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: Virtue is not a theoretical science only, but is also practical.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: The man who has been taught to obey reason will, I think, obey it whether it tells him to act or not to act.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: Life is not the way it's supposed to be. It's the way it is. The way you cope with it is what makes the difference.
author: Chrysippus

quote: Nothing happens without a cause.
author: Chrysippus

quote: I begin to speak only when I'm certain what I'll say isn't better left unsaid.
author: Cato the Younger

quote: It is part of human nature to hate the man whom you have hurt.
author: Cato the Younger

quote: Make for yourself a definition or description of the thing presented to you, so as to see distinctly what kind of a thing it is in its substance.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The soul becomes dyed with the color of its thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The art of living is more like a wrestling match than a dance.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: How free of trouble is the man who thinks of nothing else than the right action and the disposition with which it ought to be done.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do not act as if you had ten thousand years to throw away. Death stands at your elbow.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: What we do now echoes in eternity.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Every habit and capability is confirmed and grows in its corresponding actions.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Apply yourself to a problem until you understand it; you'll find that what once seemed obscure becomes simple.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Match the present moment to the demands placed upon you, and you will be at peace.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Just as you would treat your parent or teacher with deference, treat fortune the same way.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Be satisfied with success in even the smallest matter, and think that even such a result is no small thing.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: He who has felt great joy at doing someone a service is not one who looks for a profitable return.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: In your actions, don't procrastinate. In your conversations, don't confuse. In your thoughts, don't wander.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Choose not to be harmed, and you won't feel harmed. Don't feel harmed, and you haven't been.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: External things are not the problem; it's your assessment of them. Erase that and you're safe.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Be careful to attribute to the gods what belongs to the gods, and to men what belongs to men.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Each day provides its own gifts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: A man's true delight is to do the things he was made for.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: You could leave life right now. Let that determine what you do and say and think.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: If anyone can refute me, show me I'm making a mistake or looking at things from the wrong perspective, I'll gladly change.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: To bear in mind constantly that all of this has happened before, and will happen again, the same plot from beginning to end.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: When you arise in the morning, think of what a precious privilege it is to be alive, to breathe, to think, to enjoy, to love.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Think of yourself as dead. You have lived your life. Now take what's left and live it properly.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do not waste the remainder of your life thinking about others, when you are not thinking with reference to some aspect of the common good.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Take away your opinion, and then there is taken away the complaint.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do what is necessary, and whatever the reason of the animal which is naturally social requires.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: What is not good for the swarm is not good for the bee.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The opinion which we have of things is more important than the things themselves.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Let no act be done without a purpose, nor otherwise than according to the perfect principles of art.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Consider that everything which happens, happens justly, and if you observe carefully, you will find it to be so.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Look beneath the surface; let not the several qualities of a thing nor its worth escape thee.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Of human life, the time is a point, the substance is in a flux, the perception dull, the composition of the whole body subject to putrefaction.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Be not unwilling in what thou doest, neither selfish nor unreflecting nor open to distraction.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: That which rules within us, when it is according to nature, is so affected with respect to the events which happen, that it always easily adapts itself to that which is and is presented to it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Let it make no difference to thee whether thou art cold or warm, if thou art doing thy duty.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Today I escaped anxiety. Or no, I discarded it, because it was within me, in my own perceptions, not outside.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Nothing is more wretched than a man who traverses everything in a round, and pries into things beneath the earth.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: It is in thy power to live free from all compulsion in the greatest tranquility of mind.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: If thou workest at that which is before thee, following right reason seriously, vigorously, calmly, without allowing anything else to distract thee, but keeping thy divine part pure, thou wilt live happy.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Men exist for the sake of one another. Teach them then or bear with them.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Let opinion be removed, and the fear of dying is removed.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The man who does not know what the world is, does not know where he is.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: From the gods come all things full of soul, and by nature too all things are full of providence.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Look within. Within is the fountain of good, and it will ever bubble up, if thou wilt ever dig.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Unhappy am I because this has happened to me. Not so, but happy am I, though this has happened to me, because I continue free from pain.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: This is the chief thing: be not perturbed, for all things are according to the nature of the universal.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: In a little while thou wilt have forgotten everything; in a little while everything will have forgotten thee.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: It is a man's peculiar duty to love even those who wrong him.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Let men see, let them know a real man, who lives as he was meant to live.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: If you live in harmony with nature you will never be poor; if you live according to what others think, you will never be rich.
author: Seneca

quote: Throw out most of what you greet with pleasure or pain and you'll immediately find yourself in better stead.
author: Seneca

quote: The whole of life is but a point of time; let us enjoy it.
author: Seneca

quote: Sometimes there is more vitality in a glance exchanged than in hours of conversation.
author: Seneca

quote: Where the mind is gladdened, it grows fertile in invention, and that gladness must come of itself.
author: Seneca

quote: Each day acquire something that will fortify thee against poverty, against death, indeed against other misfortunes as well.
author: Seneca

quote: The greatest loss of time is delay and expectation, which depend upon the future.
author: Seneca

quote: To do philosophy is this: to test and preserve that peace within yourself and never disturb it.
author: Seneca

quote: Drunkenness is nothing but voluntary madness.
author: Seneca

quote: He who allows his day to pass by without the practice of generosity and enlargement of heart is more reprehensible than if he had been ill.
author: Seneca

quote: The willingly obedient avoid the bitterest part of slavery, doing what they had to do anyway.
author: Seneca

quote: Sometimes also there is a great pleasure in making preparations for a thing.
author: Seneca

quote: All cruelty springs from weakness.
author: Seneca

quote: Time discovers truth.
author: Seneca

quote: As is a tale, so is life: not how long it is, but how good it is, is what matters.
author: Seneca

quote: The point is, not how long you live, but how nobly you live.
author: Seneca

quote: Hold on tightly. Let go lightly. This is one of the great secrets of happiness.
author: Seneca

quote: Be silent for the most part, and exchange only such few words as are necessary, and let them be uttered with brevity.
author: Seneca

quote: True joy is a serene and dignified contentment.
author: Seneca

quote: Crescit occulto velut arbor aevo fama. Fame grows like a tree through unseen years.
author: Seneca

quote: No man is more unhappy than the one who is never in adversity; the greatest misfortune is to be exempt from misfortune.
author: Seneca

quote: Whoever does not regard what he has as ample wealth, though he be master of the whole world, is yet a man to be pitied.
author: Seneca

quote: A man who has anticipated the coming of troubles takes away their power when they arrive.
author: Seneca

quote: Nothing, to my way of thinking, is a better proof of a well-ordered mind than a man's ability to stop just where he is and pass some time in his own company.
author: Seneca

quote: All art is but imitation of nature.
author: Seneca

quote: It is quality rather than quantity that matters.
author: Seneca

quote: Be rough with yourself, at times, in your inner dialogue.
author: Seneca

quote: Calamity is virtue's opportunity.
author: Seneca

quote: Wherever there is a human being, there is an opportunity for kindness.
author: Seneca

quote: Many things which were thought impossible have through perseverance been accomplished.
author: Seneca

quote: When you close your doors and make darkness within, remember never to say that you are alone, for you are not alone.
author: Epictetus

quote: Other people's mistakes should be a source of charity rather than anger.
author: Epictetus

quote: The key is to keep company only with people who uplift you, whose presence calls forth your best.
author: Epictetus

quote: Try to enjoy the great festival of life with other men.
author: Epictetus

quote: Disease is an impediment to the body, but not to the will.
author: Epictetus

quote: It is not death or hardship that is a fearful thing, but the fear of death or hardship.
author: Epictetus

quote: Until you have made your principles firm, be cautious about associating with ordinary people.
author: Epictetus

quote: First show respect for yourself, and only then will you be able to make demands of life with confidence.
author: Epictetus

quote: Progress is not achieved by luck or accident, but by working on yourself daily.
author: Epictetus

quote: Above all, know thyself, which is the beginning of wisdom.
author: Epictetus

quote: It is not he who reviles or strikes you who insults you, but it is your opinion about these things as being insulting.
author: Epictetus

quote: Nothing great is created suddenly, any more than a bunch of grapes or a fig.
author: Epictetus

quote: Examine yourself, whether you wish to be a sea-captain or a passenger.
author: Epictetus

quote: You become what you give your attention to.
author: Epictetus

quote: He is wise who does not grieve for the things which he has not, but rejoices for those which he has.
author: Epictetus

quote: Practice yourself, for heaven's sake, in little things, and thence proceed to greater.
author: Epictetus

quote: As long as you are studying philosophy, you have nothing to fear, for nothing can be feared except a wicked life.
author: Cicero

quote: There is nothing so absurd but some philosopher has said it.
author: Cicero

quote: The wise are instructed by reason; ordinary minds by experience; the stupid by necessity; and brutes by instinct.
author: Cicero

quote: Anyone can pilot a ship in calm seas; it is the storm that reveals the captain.
author: Cicero

quote: Where there is no law, there is no freedom.
author: Cicero

quote: Justice consists in doing no injury to men; decency in giving them no offense.
author: Cicero

quote: Nature herself has placed in our minds an insatiable longing to see the truth.
author: Cicero

quote: The safety of the people is the highest law.
author: Cicero

quote: Endurance of labors is the way to good things.
author: Cicero

quote: Custom is almost a second nature.
author: Cicero

quote: Man is not worried by real problems so much as by his imagined anxieties about real problems.
author: Epictetus

quote: Choose a master whose life, conversation, and soul-expressing face have satisfied you; picture him always to yourself as your protector or your pattern.
author: Seneca

quote: We should every night call ourselves to an account: what infirmity have I mastered today? What passions opposed? What temptation resisted?
author: Seneca

quote: Set aside a certain number of days, during which you shall be content with the scantiest and cheapest fare, so that you may know by practice that poverty is no real hardship.
author: Seneca

quote: Rivers of the largest size are crossed in safety, or, if they cause fear, it is at their source.
author: Seneca

quote: Just as you would treat with deference your father, your teacher, the emperor, treat the divine reason within you the same way.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Every wrongdoer is doing harm to himself, the man who has done a wrong is the only one really harmed, since he has done harm to his own ruling faculty.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: A blazing fire makes flame and brightness out of everything that is thrown into it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Just that you do the right thing. The rest doesn't matter.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: There is nothing the wise man does reluctantly.
author: Seneca
